item.title: The more things change.... We need to make the bigots afraid again.
item.description:
Technical Sergeant Leonard Matlovich. It does not bear his name because he intended to be a monument to all gay veterans
Very interesting man. Worth going down a rabbit hole over. Here's a start: https://time.com/4019076/40-years-leonard-matlovich/
item.dc:creator: dickblack12

item.title: For RHM, I give you the lovely Lucy Darling
item.description: Lucy darling is the alter ego of Canadian magician Carissa Hendrix. She said in an interview that when she's on stage her Lucy persona takes over and that sometimes she can't believe what Lucy says.
item.dc:creator: BreakfastWithCountChocula

item.title: Memes vol 466 Cats
item.description:
Which of you sent this to that YouTuber- I mean it’s on tumblr and Pinterest as well
I love you, Arthur
Merrill
Anders
Chloe
And not long ago this phenomenal cat produced 7 kittens right out of a hat
Bug
Kittens from work
I think we’ll be taking these two
I’m taking him for sure- he’s my little son. But I’m not sure what to name him. So far I’ve been throwing around Emmett, Ducky, and Lewis. But idk the girl we’re getting I’ll be naming Miss Piggy, or Piglet and I kinda want to keep with the muppets theme
Scruffy and Froggy tax
Nessie tax
Liza Jane tax

item.title: What Happened Today #116
item.description: ? The center cannot hold when power attacks its own foundations. Trump pardons corrupt sheriffs while calling the Justice Department "corrupt," attacks education at every level from Harvard to NIH research funding, and uses Memorial Day messages to attack judges blocking his deportation orders. But resistance is everywhere: dozens of NIH scientists walk out after their new director tells them they funded studies that led to the pandemic, environmental groups file lawsuits against illegal executive orders, and federal judges keep ruling against lawless overreach. The question isn't whether democracy can survive this assault, but it's whether we'll fight hard enough to make sure it does. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/witkoff-gaza-negotiations 2. https://go.govbrief.today/deportees-djibouti-murphy 3. https://go.govbrief.today/volvo-cut-3k 4. https://go.govbrief.today/nih-staff-walkout 5. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-funds-trade-schools 6. https://go.govbrief.today/fbi-invesigating-past-cases 7. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-pardons-jenkins 8. https://go.govbrief.today/memorial-day-message YESTERDAY - https://go.govbrief.today/internior-negative-america FIGHTING BACK - https://go.govbrief.today/earthjustice-sues-fishing
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Memes/tomfoolery
item.dc:creator: stonetemplefox05

item.title: Nostalgia is the only home you get
item.description: The source is The Onion (because someone will if I don’t) I miss the ability to truly go ghost mode. The rectangle box of despair is also the rectangle box of participating in a society, can’t toss the fucker into the ocean, even though I want to 10x a day. The onion is being funny as per their job description but I do not miss the TMZ era of 00s and a bit of early 10s. I do miss the early viral content, it went viral organically, just as god intended. Gonna go rewatch Leeeeeeeroy Jenkins to relive the days of less worries.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: 1:1400 Enterprise-D. I worked on this thing on-and-off for around a year. I made a lot of mistakes, some of which I fixed, and others I couldn't.I wasn't going for screen accuracy, I just wanted it to look good. The pink hull panels were an accident, I a

.
.

item.title: In 1953, actress Jane Russell was accidentally knocked into a pool by a man diving over her — but she stayed in character and finished the scene without cutting.
